learning_outcomes:
Applicable to students admitted in 2012-13 1.  |  Major Programme:
Knowledge and comprehension
Students will have acquired the following knowledge:
(1)  |  mathematical analysis,
(2)  |  linear algebra,
(3)  |  computer programming for mathematics,
(4)  |  further knowledge in at least one direction:
-  |  (Enrichment Stream) Knowledge in mathematics enabling the students
to have solid foundation for further
studies at the graduate school level; or
-  |  (Other streams) Knowledge in a specific field together with the
mathematics applicable to that field, for example, Computational and Applied
Mathematics Stream, Computational Big Data Analytics Stream.
(5)  |  mathematical literacy sufficient for various professions, for
example, research, education, financial technology, information technology,
data science, artificial intelligence, etc.
Skills
Generic Competencies
Students will have acquired a set of generic competencies.
(1)  |  ability to understand broad mathematical literature at the
university level independently;
(2)  |  awareness of mathematical problems arising in generic situations;
(3)  |  ability to formulate such problems mathematically, to find ways to
solve them;
(4)  |  ability to communicate progress of mathematical problem solving
effectively;
(5)  |  computer literacy and knowledge to use mathematical software in
solving problems.
Sectors of Professional Skills
In addition, students should possess professional skills in at least
one sector.
(1)  |  Graduate school research
(2)  |  Teaching
(3)  |  Mathematics intensive professions
(4)  |  Financial sector
(5)  |  Science and technology sector
Values and Attitudes
Upon graduation, our students will be able to apply their mathematical
knowledge generically in their future profession in a way that is unique for
mathematically trained experts. They should be able to
(1)  |  appreciate and advocate ways of thinking with logical rigour,
(2)  |  appreciate and take advantage of quantitative and analytical
exactitude,
(3)  |  appreciate the contribution of mathematics to the improvement and
formation of modern society built upon quantitative natural sciences.
2.  |  Minor Programme:
The minor programme of study aims at providing students with good
mathematical knowledge to support their professional development in today’s
knowledge society. In brief, upon completion of the programme, we anticipate
our students to have acquired the capability of formulating and modeling
problems in a logical sound and quantitatively efficient way, which is
typical in mathematical sciences.
Knowledge and comprehension
Students will have acquired the following knowledge:
(1)  |  Calculus or linear algebra,
(2)  |  mathematical literacy sufficient for supporting a particular
profession, including but not limited to, research, education, financial
technology, information technology, data science, artificial intelligence,
etc.
(3)  |  model formulation related to a particular profession.
Skills
Students will have acquired a set of generic competencies listed
below.
(1)  |  ability to understand broad mathematical literature related to
their own professional development at the university level independently;
(2)  |  awareness of mathematical problems arising in generic situations;
(3)  |  ability to formulate such problems mathematically, to find ways to
model them;
(4)  |  ability to communicate progress of mathematical modelling
effectively.
Values and Attitudes
Upon completion of the programme, our students are expected to be
able to apply their mathematical knowledge generically in their future
profession in a way that is unique for mathematically trained experts, they
should be able to
(1)  |  appreciate, advocate, and take advantage of ways of quantitative
and analytical thinking,
(2)  |  appreciate the contribution of mathematics to the improvement and
formation of modern society built upon quantitative natural sciences.
study_scheme:
Mathematics Mathematics Applicable
to students admitted in 2023-24
Major Programme Requirement
Students are required to
complete a minimum of 71 units[a] of courses as follows:
Units
1.  |  Faculty Package:  |  9
Group C: MATH1010 or 1018 or 1030 or 1038 Group E: STAT1011 A course from the following Group A: LSCI1001 or 1002 Group B: CHEM1070 or 1072 or 1280 Group D: PHYS1001 or 1002 or 1111 or 1113
2.  |  Required Courses:  |  35
(a)  |  MATH1010 or 1018, 1030 or 1038, 1050 or 1058, 2010 or
2018, 2020 or 2028, 2040 or 2048, 2050 or 2058, 2060[b] or 2068, 2070 or
2078, 2221, 2230
(b)  |  At
least one course from MATH3060, STAT2001 or 2006
(c)  |  Capstone
course[b]: Either MATH4400 or 4900
3.  |  Elective Courses:  |  27
(a)  |  9 units of MATH courses at 3000 or above level, and
(b)  |  18 units of courses chosen according to any one
stream below:
For students who specialize in Enrichment
Stream[c]:
12-18 units from MATH3030, 3040, 3060, 3070, 3093, 3230,
3270, 3340, 4010, 4030, 4050, 4060, 4080, 4220, 5011, 5012, 5021, 5022, 5031,
5032, 5051, 5052, 5061, 5062, 5070; and 0-3 units from MATH3240, 4280; and 0-3 units from MATH3320, 4230
For students who specialize in Computational
and Applied Mathematics Stream:
CSCI1540# and MATH3230; and 9-12 units from MATH3093,
32xx, 33xx, 42xx, 43xx, in which 0-3 units may be chosen from IERG5124#,
SEEM3550#/ESTR3506#, SEEM3570#/ESTR3508#
For students who specialize in Computational
Big Data Analytics Stream:
9-12 units from MATH3320, 3330, 3340, 4280; and 3-9
units from MATH3215, 3230, 3280, 3290, 3310, 3360, 4230, 4240, in which
0-3 units may be chosen from IERG5124#, SEEM3550#/ESTR3506#, STAT4005#
For students in the Mathematics Stream:
MATH courses at 3000 or above level, and/or courses at 2000 or above
level of one other subject area[d] offered by the Faculties/Departments
of Science, Engineering, Economics and Finance, with at least 12 units of
MATH courses at 3000 or above level. The specific subject area codes are
given in explanatory note[e].
For students who specialize in Mathematics-Education
Stream:
MATH and BMED courses at 3000 or above level, with at
least 9 units of BMED courses.
For students who specialize in Mathematics-Multidisciplinary
Stream:
MATH courses at 3000 or above level, and/or courses
at 2000 or above level of at most two other subject areas[d] offered by
the Faculties/Departments of Science, Engineering, Economics, and Finance,
with at least 9 units of non-MATH courses. The specific subject area
codes are given in explanatory note[e].
Total:  |  71
In addition to fulfilling the above Major Programme
Requirement, students meeting the criteria as specified by the Faculty
can take the following stream offered by the Faculty:
Science, Technology And
Research Stream
Students are required to complete
a minimum of 12 units of courses as follows:
Units
1.  |  Required
Courses:
(a)  |  One Faculty Package Course:  |  3
Choose from the two remaining groups of the Faculty
Package that have not been used to fulfill the Faculty Package
Requirement
(b)  |  Research Courses:  |  6
STAR2000, 3000, 4000[f]
(c)  |  Seminar Courses:  |  3
STAR2050, 3050, 4050
2.  |  Experiential Learning:  |  ---
At least 4 consecutive weeks of outside Hong Kong
exposure[g]
Total:  |  12
Explanatory Notes:
1.  |  MATH1010, 1018, 1030, 1038, 1050, 1058, MATH courses
at 2000 and above level, BMED courses at 3000 and above level, courses at
2000 and above level of the subject areas listed in [e] below as well as
those labeled as # will be included in the calculation of Major GPA for
honours classification if students specialize in the respective streams.
2.  |  Students who have satisfied the requirements of both
Enrichment Stream (ESM) and Computational and Applied Mathematics Stream
(CAM) will be allowed to graduate with double stream specialization in
ESM and CAM. Students may refer to the Recommended Course Pattern
on double streams in Enrichment Stream and Computational and Applied
Mathematics Stream.
[a]  |  Students who select to graduate in Mathematics
and Information Engineering should complete a minimum of 87 units
according to the requirements of the relevant programme. They should also
declare the choice before their second year of attendance.
[b]  |  Students in Mathematics and Mathematics
Education may replace MATH2060 with any MATH course at 3000 or
above level, and take BMED4510 as Capstone course.
[c]  |  Students admitted to the Enrichment Mathematics
Programme are automatically placed in the Enrichment Stream, declaration
is not required. Other Major students may apply to specialize in the
Enrichment Stream if the requirement below is satisfied: Cumulative Major GPA of at least 3.3, or the
preceding two semesters’ Major GPA of at least 3.3.
[d]  |  Students shall declare the subject area(s) at least
one term before graduation.
[e]  |  Faculty/ Department  |  Subject Area Code
Science  |  BCHE, BIOL, CHEM, CMBI, EESC, ENSC, ESSC, FNSC, LSCI,
MBTE, PHYS, RMSC, STAT
Engineering  |  AIST, BMEG, CENG, CSCI, EEEN, ELEG, ENER, ENGG, ESTR,
FTEC, IERG, MAEG, SEEM
Economics/Finance  |  DOTE[DSME], ECON, FINA, GLEF
[f]  |  Students may select research-oriented course(s), as
approved by the Major Programme, to substitute up to 4 units for
fulfillment of Research Courses requirement.
[g]  |  Students must complete any
exchange/research/internship programme(s) offered by the University,
Colleges, the Faculty of Science or Major Programme, as approved by the
Major Programme, to fulfill the Experiential Learning requirement.
Students are responsible for the extra costs incurred in the exchange/research/internship
programme(s).
[ ]  |  Subject area code “DSME” changed to “DOTE” with
effect from 2024-25.

to graduate in the Mathematics Stream, Computational and Applied Mathematics
Stream, Computational Big Data Analytics Stream, Mathematics-Education
Stream, Mathematics-Multidisciplinary Stream or Science, Technology And
Research Stream may replace MATH1010, 1030, 1050, 2010, 2020, 2040, 2050,
2060, 2070 with MATH1018, 1038, 1058, 2018, 2028, 2048, 2058, 2068, 2078
respectively.
∞  |  Students who plan
to graduate in the Enrichment Stream or the double streams in Enrichment
Stream and Computational and Applied Mathematics Stream may replace MATH1018,
1038, 1058, 2018, 2028, 2048, 2058, 2068, 2078 with MATH1010, 1030, 1050,
2010, 2020, 2040, 2050, 2060, 2070 respectively.
*  |  Students who plan
to graduate in Mathematics and Information Engineering or Mathematics
and Mathematics Education should follow the Recommended Course
Pattern of the corresponding programmes.
